arts.sum: This week in Lazio history we remember some fantastic wins, another Caicedo last second goal, the 2019 Supercoppa and the passing of Sinisa Mihajlovic. arts.ttl: Serie A | Lecce v Lazio: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Lazio's fortunes have taken a positive turn ahead of their upcoming Serie A fixture against Lecce. Despite a disastrous 6-0 defeat to Inter Milan on Monday, which saw Samuel Gigot and Mario Gila forced off with injuries, both centre-backs have made swift recoveries and are in contention to feature this weekend. The Serie A clash takes place at the Via del Mare Stadium on Saturday, 20:45 local time. arts.ttl: Lecce to host Lazio at 20:45 CET
arts.sum: Lazio have won 18 matches against Lecce in Serie A (D7 L9): a 53%-win percentage; among the teams they’ve faced at least 30 times in the competition, the Biancocelesti have a higher percentage of wins only against Cagliari (55%) and Livorno (61%). Lecce have won each of their last three Serie A home games against Lazio, only against Cagliari have they won four home matches in a row in the competition between 2000 and 2008.
On 9 January 2025, Lazio will celebrate its 125th anniversary.

arts.ttl: Napoli’s surprise move for former Juventus star Hasa
arts.sum: Napoli has begun to make significant moves in the transfer market as they prepare for the January window. The club is not only focusing on high-profile signings for manager Antonio Conte but is also looking to invest in promising young talents who could benefit from first-team experience. arts.ttl: Corvino: ‘Lecce won’t part with Dorgu in January despite Napoli Interest’
arts.sum: Pantaleo Corvino, Lecce's technical director, addressed the media today alongside sporting director Trinchera to outline the club's approach to the upcoming January transfer window. Corvino emphasized the unique nature of the winter market, describing it as a "repair market" with its own timelines and easily understood rules. The post Corvino: ‘Lecce won’t part with Dorgu in January despite Napoli Interest’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.
